summary: High‑energy NYE set with monster Good Lovin’→Samson and epic Help/Slip/NFA/Dew
blurb: The second set erupts with a thunderous Good Lovin' → Samson transition, a sprawling Help On The Way > Slipknot > Not Fade Away > Morning Dew jam, and a tight Scarlet Begonias. Jerry’s guitar and vocal work are on fire while Phil drives the deep improvisations. This show shines on the clear FM broadcast recording.
review: December 31, 1976 at the Cow Palace delivered one of the Dead’s most electrifying New Year's Eve performances. The first set laid a solid groundwork with classics like The Promised Land and Playing In The Band, but it was the marathon second set that truly dazzled: Sugar Magnolia segued into Eyes Of The World, then into Wharf Rat before the drums opened the door for an unforgettable Good Lovin' → Samson & Delilah groove. From there the band launched into a blistering Help On The Way > Slipknot > Not Fade Away > Morning Dew excursion that many fans cite as a highlight of the ’76 season, while Scarlet Begonias provided a crisp, melodic counter‑point. Jerry’s tone and vocal “DEW melt‑down” were relentless, Phil’s bass thumped front‑and‑center, and Keith’s keyboards added texture throughout. The dual drummers cooked a powerful backbeat that kept the momentum rolling to the final encore of Uncle John’s Band and a heartfelt Good Night. For anyone wanting to hear this night at its clearest, the FM broadcast capture (gd1976-12-31.fm.hollister.15114.shnf) offers a well‑balanced mix that even outshines the official release.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the show’s high energy, monster jams (Good Lovin'→Samson, Help/Slip/NFA/Dew) and tight band interplay. Multiple reviewers use language like "blistering", "relentless", "legendary" and highlight the same standout moments, warranting a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Clear FM broadcast with a solid, well‑balanced mix that captures the high‑energy performance and is clearer than the official release despite minor speed issues.

key_highlights: Extended Help On The Way > Slipknot > Not Fade Away > Morning Dew jam
Tight Scarlet Begonias segues into a crisp jam
Funky Playing In The Band and deep second‑set improvisations

band_performance.Jerry: His tone and guitar work are praised as relentless and soaring, with a standout vocal melt‑down on the jam sections.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is front‑and‑center, thumping through Playing In The Band and driving the deep improvisations, especially the Slipknot jam.
band_performance.Bob: Works the edges during Slipknot, adds vocal power on Morning Dew, and provides solid rhythm throughout.
band_performance.Keith: Provides A‑game keyboard textures, complementing Jerry’s leads; early timing quirks give way to higher‑note flourishes that color the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Delivers powerful vamping and collective percussion that fuels the second‑set transitions.
band_performance.Billy: Offers cooking backbeat and brief drum solos, keeping the groove tight through the extended jams.
